quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 5 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Comparing and Contrasting Circumcenter, Incenter, Centroid, and Orthocenter
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: Which point of concurrency is formed by the intersection of the three medians?
questions.explanation: The centroid is the point where the three medians intersect. It is the center of mass of the triangle and divides each median in a $2:1$ ratio.

questions.question: Which point of concurrency is equidistant from all three vertices of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The circumcenter is equidistant from the three vertices. This equal distance is the radius of the circumcircle that passes through all three vertices.

questions.question: Which point of concurrency is equidistant from all three sides of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The incenter is equidistant from the three sides. This equal distance is the radius of the incircle that is tangent to all three sides.

questions.question: Which point of concurrency can lie outside the triangle?
options: Both the circumcenter and the orthocenter
Both the centroid and the incenter
questions.explanation: The circumcenter and orthocenter lie outside the triangle when the triangle is obtuse. The centroid and incenter are always inside the triangle.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the circumcenter is located at:
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the circumcenter is at the midpoint of the hypotenuse. This is because the hypotenuse is a diameter of the circumcircle.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the orthocenter is located at:
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two legs serve as altitudes. They intersect at the right angle vertex, making that vertex the orthocenter.

questions.question: Which point of concurrency divides each of its associated segments in a $2:1$ ratio?
questions.explanation: The centroid divides each median so that the distance from the vertex to the centroid is twice the distance from the centroid to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle, which statement is true?
options: All four points of concurrency coincide at the same point
Only the centroid and orthocenter coincide
Only the circumcenter and incenter coincide
All four points are located at different positions
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, the circumcenter, incenter, centroid, and orthocenter all coincide at the same point due to the perfect symmetry of the triangle.

questions.question: Which points of concurrency are always located inside the triangle, regardless of triangle type?
questions.explanation: The centroid and incenter are always inside the triangle for all triangle types — acute, right, and obtuse. The circumcenter and orthocenter can be outside for obtuse triangles.

questions.question: A point is the intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of the sides. It is also the center of the circumcircle. Which point is it?
questions.explanation: The circumcenter is the intersection of the perpendicular bisectors and serves as the center of the circumscribed circle that passes through all three vertices.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can clearly distinguish all four triangle centers, their associated segments, their distance properties, and their locations in different triangle types.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main centers but review which ones can lie outside the triangle and where they are located in right triangles specifically.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: circumcenter (perpendicular bisectors, equidistant from vertices), incenter (angle bisectors, equidistant from sides), centroid (medians, $2:1$ ratio), orthocenter (altitudes).
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Memorize the four centers: Circumcenter = perpendicular bisectors, Incenter = angle bisectors, Centroid = medians, Orthocenter = altitudes. Only in an equilateral triangle do they all coincide.
